perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data, or know -how (including, but not limited to, information relating to research, products, software, services, development, inv entions, processes, engineering, marketing, techniques, customers, pricing, internal procedures, business and marketing plans or strategies, finances, employees and busi ness opportunities) disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient, either directly or indirectly, in any form whatsoever (including, but not limited to, in writing, in machine readable or other tangible form, orally or visually): (i) that is a trade secret u nder applicable law; (ii) that has been marked as “confidential” or “proprietary ” or similar legend; (iii) whose confidential nature has been made known by the Disclosing Party, orally or in writing, to the Recipient; (iv) that due to its character and nature, a reasonable person under like circumstances would treat as confidential; o r (v) discussions relating to such information whether these discussions occur prior to, concurrent with, or following disclosure of such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data, or know -how (including, but not limited to, information relating to research, products, software, services, development, inv entions, processes, engineering, marketing, techniques, customers, pricing, internal procedures, business and marketing plans or strategies, finances, employees and busi ness opportunities) disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient, either directly or indirectly, in any form whatsoever (including, but not limited to, in writing, in machine readable or other tangible form, orally or visually): (i) that is a trade secret u nder applicable law; (ii) that has been marked as “confidential” or “proprietary ” or similar legend; (iii) whose confidential nature has been made known by the Disclosing Party, orally or in writing, to the Recipient; (iv) that due to its character and nature, only the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, considers confidential; o r (v) discussions relating to such information whether these discussions occur prior to, concurrent with, or following disclosure of such information.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying subsection (iv) of the definition of 'Confidential Information', we've created an in-text contradiction. Originally, information was considered confidential if a 'reasonable person' would treat it as such. Now, it's based solely on the 'Disclosing Party's sole discretion.' This contradicts the general understanding that confidentiality should have an objective standard. This introduces ambiguity because the Recipient is later obligated to treat certain information as confidential, and this standard is now based on the 'Disclosing party sole discretion'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees that it shall treat the Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own confidential and proprietary information of a similar nature; provided that in no event shall the R ecipient exercise less than reasonable care to protect the Confidential Information commensurate with the sensitivity of such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined confidential information based on what a 'reasonable person under like circumstances would treat as confidential.' The changed text alters this to base it solely on the 'Disclosing Party's sole discretion.' This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states the Recipient must treat the information with 'reasonable care to protect the Confidential Information commensurate with the sensitivity of such information.' The 'reasonable care' standard implies an objective measure, conflicting with the new subjective 'Disclosing Party's sole discretion' standard.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient understands and agrees that the Disclosing Party is providing the Confidential Informat ion to the Recipient in reliance upon this Agreement, and the Recipient will be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any damage s or harm caused to the Disclosing Party by a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient understands and agrees that the Disclosing Party is providing the Confidential Informat ion to the Recipient in reliance upon this Agreement, and the Recipient will only be responsible for direct damages proven to be a result of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Recipient or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original text holds the Recipient fully responsible for *any* damages caused by a breach. The change limits responsibility to only *direct damages* caused by *gross negligence or willful misconduct*. This introduces ambiguity because, in other sections, the Recipient's obligations are stated without these limitations. This creates a contradiction with the initial agreement of full responsibility and the later limitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient (i) agrees not to use the Confidential Information for its own use or for any purposes except the Authorized Purpose expressly set forth above; (ii) shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of unfair or improper competition; and (iii) agrees not to copy, alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any of the materials unless permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party (any copies that are authorized to be made will be identified as belonging to th e Disclosing Party and marked as “confidential”, “proprietary” or with similar legend).
perturbation.justification: The original text held the Recipient fully responsible for any damages caused by a breach. The changed text limits responsibility to direct damages resulting from gross negligence or willful misconduct. The contradicted text states that the Recipient agrees not to use Confidential Information, compete unfairly, or copy materials. The changed text now limits the Recipient's liability, contradicting the original agreement and creating a contradiction. Specifically, the original text implied full liability for *any* breach of the non-use and non-disclosure obligations, while the modified text limits liability only to situations of gross negligence or willful misconduct. Therefore, if the Recipient breaches the agreement in a way that is not due to gross negligence or willful misconduct but still causes damage, the changed text contradicts the expectation of full responsibility.

perturbation.original_text: Any materials or documents of the Disclosing Party that are furnished to the Recipient, and all copies thereof, at the earlier of the Disclosing Party’s request for return of the materials, or the termination of the business relationship between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient, at the Disclosing Party’s option, will either be (subject to the Disclosing P arty’s reasonable instructions): (i) promptl y returned to the Disclosing Party and/or (ii) destroyed by the Recipient, including materials stored on the Recipient’s computing environment, or electronic storage media, to the extent technically feasible (with the Recipient providing written certification of such destruction).
perturbation.changed_text: Any materials or documents of the Disclosing Party that are furnished to the Recipient, and all copies thereof, at the earlier of the Disclosing Party’s request for return of the materials, or the termination of the business relationship between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient, will be retained by the Recipient for archival purposes unless the Disclosing Party provides written authorization for destruction. At the Disclosing Party’s option, the materials may be returned.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of materials at the Disclosing Party's option. The modified text states that the Recipient retains the materials for archival purposes unless explicitly authorized for destruction. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the handling of the materials upon request or termination. This contradicts the original instruction about materials, as they are no longer up to the discretion of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications among the parties that are made from the date Confidential Inf ormation was first provided to or obtained by the Recipient (even if prior to the Effective Date) through the date on which a party receives from the other party written notice that such party is terminating this Agreement. The Recipient’s obligations und er this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall continue until as long as the Confidential Information remains a trade secret or for a period of five (5) years after the dat e of disclos ure, whichever is later.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the handling of materials upon request or termination of the agreement. The original text allows the Disclosing Party to choose between return or destruction of the materials. The changed text mandates retention by the Recipient unless the Disclosing Party provides explicit authorization for destruction, contradicting the original discretion and conflicting with the contradicted text that outlines the agreement continues until termination of the agreement is provided.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third parties or to any of its employees, dire ctors, officers, agents, contractors, consultants, advisors, or other representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”), except those Representatives who have a need to know the Confidential Information for accomplishing the Authorized Purpose described her ein. In such instance(s), the Representatives (i) shall be made aware that the Confidential Information is confidential; and (ii) that such Representatives’ disclosure of the Confidential Information is restricted as set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees to disclose the Confidential Information only to those third parties or to any of its employees, dire ctors, officers, agents, contractors, consultants, advisors, or other representatives (collectively “ Representatives ”) whom the Recipient deems necessary to involve for accomplishing the Authorized Purpose described her ein. In such instance(s), the Representatives (i) shall be made aware that the Confidential Information is confidential; and (ii) that such Representatives’ disclosure of the Confidential Information is restricted as set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Recipient agrees 'not to disclose' confidential information except to those with a 'need to know'. The change modifies this to 'to disclose' only to those deemed 'necessary to involve' by the Recipient. This introduces a contradiction. It changes the Recipient's obligation from a restrictive one of minimizing disclosure to an affirmative one of disclosing to anyone the Recipient deems necessary. This creates an ambiguity about the level of confidentiality required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient understands and agrees that the Disclosing Party is providing the Confidential Informat ion to the Recipient in reliance upon this Agreement, and the Recipient will be fully responsible to the Disclosing Party for any damage s or harm caused to the Disclosing Party by a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or any of its Representatives. T he Recipient acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any of its promises or agreements contained herein will result in irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party for which there will be no adequate remedy at law, and the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunctive relief, in the event of any breach or threatened breach or intended breach of t his Agreement by the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts disclosure of confidential information, while the changed text mandates disclosure to anyone the Recipient deems 'necessary'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the Recipient is fully responsible for any breach of the agreement, as the changed text significantly broadens the scope of permissible disclosure, increasing the risk of a breach and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change introduces ambiguity about the level of confidentiality required, breaking the reliance the Disclosing Party has on the Recipient.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party shall retain the sole ownership and right to possess its Confidential Information, and any derivatives thereof. No license is granted to the Recipient under any patents, copyrights, mask w ork rights or other proprietary rights by the disclosure of any information hereunder, nor is any warranty made as to such information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party shall retain the sole ownership and right to possess its Confidential Information, and any derivatives thereof; however, the Recipient shall be granted a non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes. No further license is granted to the Recipient under any patents, copyrights, mask w ork rights or other proprietary rights by the disclosure of any information hereunder, nor is any warranty made as to such information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that 'No license is granted to the Recipient'. The changed text grants a 'non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes'. This is a direct contradiction. It creates ambiguity about the scope of the Recipient's rights regarding the Confidential Information. The lack of license originally ensured compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient (i) agrees not to use the Confidential Information for its own use or for any purposes except the Authorized Purpose expressly set forth above; (ii) shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of unfair or improper competition; and (iii) agrees not to copy, alter, modify, disassemble, reverse engineer or decompile any of the materials unless permitted in writing by the Disclosing Party (any copies that are authorized to be made will be identified as belonging to th e Disclosing Party and marked as “confidential”, “proprietary” or with similar legend).
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants a 'non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research and development purposes', while the contradicted text states the Recipient 'agrees not to use the Confidential Information for its own use or for any purposes except the Authorized Purpose expressly set forth above'. The original text explicitly states that 'No license is granted to the Recipient'. Therefore, there is a direct contradiction because the changed text now provides a license that was originally prohibited, directly conflicting with the Recipient's obligation in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications among the parties that are made from the date Confidential Inf ormation was first provided to or obtained by the Recipient (even if prior to the Effective Date) through the date on which a party receives from the other party written notice that such party is terminating this Agreement. The Recipient’s obligations und er this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall continue until as long as the Confidential Information remains a trade secret or for a period of five (5) years after the dat e of disclos ure, whichever is later.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications among the parties that are made from the date Confidential Inf ormation was first provided to or obtained by the Recipient (even if prior to the Effective Date) through the date on which a party receives from the other party written notice that such party is terminating this Agreement. The Recipient’s obligations und er this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information it has received or obtained prior to such termination shall cease immediately, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties at the time of termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the Recipient's obligations continue after termination for the trade secret's lifetime or five years. The altered clause states these obligations cease immediately upon termination, unless otherwise agreed in writing. This is a direct contradiction about the duration of confidentiality obligations post-termination, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality. The continuation was removed so it is in contradiction with the definition of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information ” means any information, technical data, or know -how (including, but not limited to, information relating to research, products, software, services, development, inv entions, processes, engineering, marketing, techniques, customers, pricing, internal procedures, business and marketing plans or strategies, finances, employees and busi ness opportunities) disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient, either directly or indirectly, in any form whatsoever (including, but not limited to, in writing, in machine readable or other tangible form, orally or visually): (i) that is a trade secret u nder applicable law; (ii) that has been marked as “confidential” or “proprietary ” or similar legend; (iii) whose confidential nature has been made known by the Disclosing Party, orally or in writing, to the Recipient; (iv) that due to its character and nature, only the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, considers confidential; o r (v) discussions relating to such information whether these discussions occur prior to, concurrent with, or following disclosure of such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a contradiction because it states that the Recipient's obligations regarding Confidential Information cease immediately upon termination unless otherwise agreed in writing, whereas the contradicted text defines Confidential Information, implying its protection extends beyond termination, particularly for trade secrets. This contradicts the defined nature and expected handling of 'Confidential Information' by removing the original clause which provided for continuation of obligations after termination for a specified period.
